tests.summary: Update order — valid status transition via state machine
tests.description: PATCH advances order status through a transition allowed by state-machine; invalid transitions are rejected.
tests.notes: PASS — Executed automated test matrix against PATCH /api/orders/[order_number] on development. 9 of 10 transitions verified successfully (1 skipped due to test ordering). All 5 valid transitions (ACTIVE→ASSIGNED→IN_PROGRESS→DELIVERED→COMPLETED, PENDING→CANCELLED) returned 200 and persisted in DB. All 4 invalid transitions tested (skip-steps and terminal-state reversals) returned 422 with "Cannot transition status from X to Y" and DB state remained unchanged (rollback verified). State machine in src/lib/state-machine/order-state.ts working as designed.

tests.summary: Reject edit when order is in terminal status
tests.description: PATCH on COMPLETED / DELIVERED / CANCELLED orders is rejected; record unchanged.
tests.notes: PASS — Verified via UI on development.readysetllc.com as ADMIN. Attempted edits on orders in COMPLETED, DELIVERED, and CANCELLED states were rejected as expected; no changes persisted. Edit guard from isTerminalStatus() in src/app/api/orders/[order_number]/schemas.ts (TERMINAL_STATUSES = COMPLETED, DELIVERED, CANCELLED) correctly blocks field mutations on terminal orders. State machine in order-state.ts is independent and governs status transitions only.

tests.description: Bulk-delete soft-deletes multiple orders and cleans associated uploaded files from S3.
tests.notes: FAIL — Code review (2026-05-08). 6 defects in src/app/api/orders/bulk-delete/route.ts: (1) hard delete instead of soft (lines 196-204); (2) wrong bucket name "order-files" vs actual "fileUploader"; (3) BUCKET_NAME shadowed; (4) path extraction fallback returns only filename → orphans; (5) storage errors swallowed (transaction commits anyway); (6) no orphan tracking. Drift from delete/route.ts singular handler. Bug ticket: REA-343

tests.description: Assigning a driver updates order, creates Delivery row with assignedAt, and broadcasts driver status to admin dashboard.
tests.notes: FAIL — Code review (2026-05-08). Steps 1-2 PASS with caveats; Step 3 FAIL: no realtime broadcast emitted from assignDriver/route.ts. Infrastructure exists (broadcastDeliveryAssigned in lib/realtime/channels.ts:475) but is dead code — 0 callers. Admin dashboard requires reload. Secondary: Delivery upsert creates rows with deliveryAddress='' and swallows errors silently. See REA-344

tests.description: Missing pickup or delivery address (or required datetimes) fails create with field error and no order is created.
tests.notes: FAIL - Critical (highest of 5 defects) 5 (1 Critical, 2 High, 2 Medium). D1: form ignores server fieldErrors. D2: dead-code submit paths bypass Zod. D3: server action input typed as z.output. D4: Zod↔Prisma drift on datetimes (verified via psql — nullable in DB despite Zod required; 0 dirty rows in prod, structural only). D5: address shows only first subfield error. See Plane work flow REA-345

steps.expected: Field error returned; no order created
Field error returned; no order created
Field error returned; no order created

tests.description: needHost=YES requires hoursNeeded and numberOfHosts; needHost=NO requires both null.
tests.notes: PARTIAL PASS.- High (highest of 3 defects). D1: Step 3 unreachable from canonical UI flow — host fields conditionally hidden + autoreset on needHost=NO. D2: dead-code submit paths (manualDirectSubmit, debugSubmit) silently coerce hoursNeeded/numberOfHosts to null instead of erroring. D3: duplicated cleanup logic across useEffect and Select onValueChange (code smell). DB alignment verified via psql — no Zod↔Postgres drift on needHost/hoursNeeded/numberOfHosts. See REA-346

tests.summary: Role gating — HELPDESK PATCH; ADMIN-only DELETE
tests.description: HELPDESK can update orders but cannot delete; ADMIN/SUPER_ADMIN can do both.
tests.notes: PASS (role gating) / FAIL (collateral). Critical (D1: hard delete)D1: DELETE hard-deletes (REA-OMG-06 reincidence), verified via psql — only 1 of 83 catering_requests has deletedAt populated. D2: PATCH status-only updates bypass role gating (any authenticated user can change order status). D3: profile lookup uses Supabase in PATCH vs Prisma in DELETE. D4: PATCH uses order_number, DELETE uses orderId UUID. D5: no HELPDESK delete-request path for future role evolution. D6: soft-delete schema incomplete (only deletedAt; missing deletedBy and deletionReason). See REA-347

tests.summary: Driver shift broadcasts on driver-locations channel (realtime ON)
tests.description: With both feature flags ON, a driver going on-shift broadcasts location updates over Supabase Realtime channel driver-locations.
tests.notes: PASS (with concerns).- High (D1 ticket data, D2 validation drift)D1: ticket Test Data lists FF_* env vars without required NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix; copying literally would disable flags. D2: validateBroadcastPayload validStatuses array missing 'EN_ROUTE_TO_VENDOR' (compounds REA-OMG-08). D3: channels.ts docstring references migration 20251107000008 which does not exist (verified via filesystem). D4: DriverLocationPayload includes activityType but driver_locations table has no matching column — broadcast preserves it, REST persistence drops it. D5: dual transport (broadcast + postgres_changes) without documented dedup contract — admin listener may receive duplicate events. D6: FF_USE_REALTIME_ADMIN_DASHBOARD listed as required test data but only affects admin receiver, not driver broadcaster. DB verified via psql (driver_locations 15 columns, no activity_type), migration history grep confirmed missing trigger migration. See REA-348

steps.step: Sign in as driver; open /driver/tracking; start shift
steps.expected: Portal subscribes to driver-locations; geolocation accepted
driver:location:updated events emitted; visible in WS frames
Driver appears live on map without refresh

tests.summary: REST fallback when realtime is OFF
tests.description: With realtime flags OFF, driver location reaches admin via /api/tracking/locations + /api/tracking/live polling.
tests.notes: FAIL — Static review + DB introspection on development (project khvteminrbghoeuqajzm, branch development @ 1d2e8d10). 9 defects (1 Critical, 5 High, 1 Medium, 2 Low). Test premise contradicts real architecture: client never calls POST /api/tracking/locations (0 callsites in src/, useLocationTracking calls server action updateDriverLocation in driver-actions.ts:392); /api/tracking/live serves text/event-stream (SSE), not polling; FF_USE_REALTIME_*=false are code defaults (feature-flags.ts), not test config. Critical finding (DT2-09): repo↔DB drift. Migrations 20251107000001/000005/000006/000007/000008 and 20260109000001 not registered in supabase_migrations.schema_migrations under those names; trigger trigger_driver_location_update and function notify_driver_location_update don't exist in development DB (verified via pg_trigger and pg_proc). Comment in driver-actions.ts:463 ("Database trigger automatically broadcasts") is factually false — likely root cause of DT-001. POST endpoint orphan, no auth, middleware excludes /api from matcher, would INSERT activity_type column that doesn't exist in runtime schema. RLS enabled on driver_locations but mitigation depends on whether node-pg Pool runs as service_role. Recommendation: delete orphan endpoint (closes 5 defects in 1 PR), apply migration 000008 to development, rewrite test to validate real architecture (server action + SSE), reconcile repo↔DB before any further QA in this layer.

tests.description: Each delivery stage (assigned -> delivered) persists timestamps and updates admin dashboard without manual refresh.
tests.notes: FAIL — DT6-04 Critical (case-sensitive filter omits COMPLETED, terminal deliveries stay "active"), DT6-13 Critical (order# is UUID substring + hardcoded "On Demand", not searchable), DT6-12 Critical (Sistemas A/B parallel, no FK), DT6-19 Critical (Complete accepts no proof of delivery), DT6-02 Critical (no Realtime listener for delivery rows, only SSE 5s polling works). Driver-side persistence PASS (7/7 timestamps). Admin propagation PARTIAL (via SSE, not Realtime). 20 findings total (5 Critical, 8 High, 5 Medium, 2 Low). Blocks production release. See REA-368 on Plane.
